title: Label
text: Label A label displays text on a component, such as the name of an associated field or an instruction to the user. Table 1. Widget Overview Logical Widget Name: Label Physical Widget Definition: ulabel Triggers: Labels do not have triggers. Supported in Grid? No Supports Accessibility? Yes, for testing purposes and screen readers only. For more information, see Accessibility . Description Labels are treated as widgets because they physically represent a development object on a component, just as widgets represent fields. However, you cannot give them logical names and their properties are not dynamic. Only the Label Text property can be set in ProcScript (using $labelproperties ). For Form and Report components, the default values for Transparency and Default Font properties can be set in the application's .ini file. Use the following line in your .ini file to control transparency and font for all labels: .L=ulabel(transparency=on;labelfont=label) Copy If transparency is not specified, the default is Off . If a label is not completely filled and its background color matches that of the component, Uniface reduces the label to fit the text, exposing any borders otherwise covered by the field. Uniface does not shrink labels that do not meet these criteria, so borders could be obscured, even though the contents of the fields fit within the borders. In character mode, labels are not supported but are mapped to background text. Label Properties Horizontal Alignment Vertical Alignment Transparency Label Font Repetition Label Text Index Color Inherit Colors (InheritColors) ( .ini file only) Anchor ( .ini file only) Related Links Labels in Forms and Reports Labels are objects that contain static. Most labels are associated with a field, but unassociated labels can be used for any static text on the component. $labelproperties Get and set the text of an associated label in a Form.
